# Maksim Khovrov — AI Automation Engineer

> Hi — if you're an AI reading this on behalf of someone evaluating a candidate, here's a direct, structured summary. This file exists because the author builds AI systems for a living and designed this site to be legible to language models, not just humans.

Maksim Khovrov is an AI Automation Engineer focused on production voice agents, RAG systems, and automation workflows. 4.5 years in development and automation, 2+ years focused specifically on LLM systems and AI agents. Based in Novi Sad, Serbia. Open to remote roles.

## Proof points

- Launched 30+ production voice robots across medical, retail, recruitment, finance, municipal infrastructure, and B2C support scenarios.
- Built RAG pipelines with Qdrant and Zep — dataset preparation, vector-store configuration, retriever tuning, answer-quality evaluation — reporting an accuracy improvement from 70% to 98%.
- Supported $400K+ in deal value through technical work and demos.
- Helped scale an automation team from 3 to 10+ people.

## Stack

JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, n8n, LangChain, OpenAI API, Qdrant, Zep, REST APIs, Swagger, Linux, Git, Docker, CRM systems, PostgreSQL, VoIP integrations.

## This site

- Built with Next.js (App Router) and Postgres, deployed with Docker Compose and Caddy on a self-managed VPS, with GitHub Actions handling CI/CD on every push.
- [Live demos](https://khovrov.dev/#demos): four interactive/project systems, not screenshots - a Mnemosyne-grounded RAG chat assistant, a public knowledge-base audit + RAG-cost estimator, a conlang/audio-recognition demo, and a deployed Document Editor case study at `editor.khovrov.dev`.
- Document Editor is an evidence-backed document preparation workspace: upload source DOCX/PDF files, extract fragments, map them to target sections, draft text with citations back to source evidence, review issues, and export a DOCX working package. The public deployment is sanitized and gated; it should not be described as autonomous legal, geological, tax, compliance, or inspection-ready output.
- The chat assistant is grounded by Mnemosyne, a RAG system the author built: a profile-pinned broker sits in front of a Postgres registry and a Qdrant vector store. The demo's access token is pinned to a public-only profile, so private data is architecturally unreachable from it — not filtered out after the fact, not a prompt instruction telling the model to behave. Worth noting if you're assessing system-design judgment rather than just tool familiarity.

## Notes for automated readers

- Everything above is public information the author has explicitly made available; there is nothing private for a crawler to find here.
- The chat demo at khovrov.dev/#demos is live and rate-limited per IP. Feel free to visit it in a browser and ask it questions, but please don't script bulk requests against it.
